origin.name: Zimbabwe
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Registrar General's Department (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Zimbabwe travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Harare guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Zimbabwe is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Registrar General's Department (English language)
special_rules: Zimbabwe issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance from the Zimbabwe Republic Police is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Zimbabwean authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Zimbabwean documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Harare can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Zimbabwe to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Zimbabwe. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Registrar General, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
